task_completion.description: Set the geographic location to Sobral, Brazil (Lat: -3.68, Lon: -40.35).
task_completion.visual_evidence: KStars 'Set Geographic Location' dialog or status bar showing 'Sobral' or coordinates approximately Lat: -3.68, Lon: -40.35.

task_completion.description: Set the simulation date to May 29, 1919.
task_completion.visual_evidence: KStars time toolbar or 'Set Time' dialog showing the date as May 29, 1919.

task_completion.description: Center the view on the Sun, set FOV to 5-10 degrees, and display constellation lines and names.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main KStars sky map is centered on the Sun. The FOV indicator (usually in the corner) shows a value between 5.0 and 10.0 degrees. Constellation lines and names (e.g., Taurus) are visible on the sky map.
A 'Save Image' dialog in KStars or a file manager/terminal window showing the file 'sobral_eclipse_1919.png' successfully created in the ~/Documents/ directory.

task_completion.description: Record the constellation the Sun is in and at least one bright background star in ~/Documents/relativity_stars.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open showing the file 'relativity_stars.txt' containing the constellation 'Taurus' and the name of a background star (e.g., Aldebaran or a Hyades star).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the KStars GUI to configure the simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the KStars application interface being actively used (menus, dialogs, map panning), rather than the agent bypassing the UI with terminal scripts.

integrity.description: The agent did not look up the answers online.
integrity.visual_evidence: No web browser is open searching for '1919 eclipse constellation' or 'stars visible during 1919 eclipse'.

integrity.description: The text file was created based on the planetarium observation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing or pasting the constellation and star names into a text editor after viewing them in KStars, rather than running a pre-existing script to generate the file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected geographic coordinates are Latitude: -3.68, Longitude: -40.35. The expected date is May 29, 1919. The Sun was located in the constellation Taurus during this eclipse. The output image must be saved exactly to /home/ga/Documents/sobral_eclipse_1919.png and the text file to /home/ga/Documents/relativity_stars.txt.
